summary: Taxonomy mapping is complete and non-blocking for this scoped provenance repair. The candidate updates existing D+Q bone finding, outcome, and result records with text-snapshot provenance only and does not create new hallmark, track, intervention, source, study, outcome, or result identities.

findings.claim_or_issue: The candidate ledger contains one candidate_change record plus updates to one existing finding, seven existing outcomes, and seven existing results.
findings.why_it_matters: A provenance-repair candidate should not silently expand the evidence graph or introduce new taxonomy-bearing evidence identities.
findings.recommended_action: Route any future new outcome, result, source, study, hallmark, track, or intervention identity through a separate extraction or taxonomy candidate.

findings.claim_or_issue: The reviewed records remain under the senolytics and dasatinib-quercetin scope, with no new source or study identifiers introduced by the provenance repair.
findings.why_it_matters: Stable identifiers prevent downstream evidence maps from treating a locator repair as a new evidence body.
findings.recommended_action: Keep this candidate limited to provenance linkage and section-stable locators.
